intro: Synnema africanum, also known as African Synnema, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are said to aid in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Synnema africanum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Synnema africanum is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
